name: Winston Lin
title: Product, platform, and operating work
tagline: I work across cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems. Most of my time goes to the part of the job where product judgment, execution, and operating detail all meet: product bets, workflow design, rollout work, and the tools around them. That usually means working across engineers, operators, leadership, and business stakeholders. I also advise and invest in a small set of businesses.
meta_description: Case studies, writing, and practical tools from cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and operating work.

oracle_blog_links.title: OCI Functions in Customer Workflows: Common Patterns and Practical Design Checklists
A Practical Guide to Cross-Region Recovery for OCI Functions
Introducing Long-Running Functions and Response Destinations with OCI Functions

intro_paragraphs: I’ve worked across cloud products, developer platforms, AI workflows, and business systems, and I also work with operators and founders on growth and service design.
experience_highlights: Principal Product Manager for OCI Functions, focused on developer experience and execution model
Previously led product work across OCI DevOps / CI/CD and adjacent platform systems
Built internal platform capabilities that shortened delivery cycles and reduced operational drag

intro: I'm based in San Francisco and work across cloud products, developer tools, AI workflows, and business systems. Most of my work has lived in the part of the job where direction, constraints, and day-to-day execution all meet.

I like getting from abstraction to something real: a clearer workflow, a sharper decision, a better handoff, or a small tool that saves people time. I use AI pretty hands-on as part of that, usually with real files, scripts, and review loops rather than one-off prompting.

I'm comfortable working across engineers, operators, leadership, and business stakeholders. Outside the day job, I also advise and invest in a small set of businesses and startups in the U.S. and Asia.
background: I grew up between Taipei and Orange County, which made me comfortable moving between different environments early.

I later earned a B.S. from UC Berkeley Haas and got more interested in the overlap between business and technology. I also spent a lot of time tutoring, mentoring, and working in lab assistant roles, and stayed involved after college as a mentor in the undergraduate program through 2025.
Competitive math and community volunteering were also a big part of how I grew up. That mix of analytical work and helping people still shapes how I think and how I like to work.
personal: Outside of work, I like being outdoors, hiking around the Bay Area, and shooting on Fujifilm.

I grew up playing sports and still play basketball and volleyball when I can. More recently I’ve gotten into running and Barry's classes. I’m also a big anime fan, especially Frieren and Your Lie in April, and I spend time thinking about investing, real estate, and long-term bets on businesses.
